action.reasoning: Observation: Iterations 2-3 failed catastrophically (1.2-1.6% accuracy) while iteration 1's simple approach got 74.84%. The root cause is the LR scheduler bug: scheduler.step() was called per-batch but T_max was set to num_epochs (not total_steps), causing the cosine schedule to cycle through ~23 full cycles per epoch, driving LR to near-zero almost immediately. This explains why val_acc stayed at ~1% (random for 100 classes) despite declining train_loss. Analysis: Need to fix the scheduler by using LambdaLR with proper step counting (total_steps = steps_per_epoch * num_epochs). Also, need to save EMA weights (not training weights) as the model_state_dict in the checkpoint. Decision: Write clean training script with proper cosine LR schedule + warmup, ConvNeXt-Tiny, mixup/cutmix/label_smoothing/EMA, train 30 epochs, submit eval immediately.
